steps.title: Simplify the ratio
steps.content:
The length-to-width ratio is $45$ to $12$. Divide both values by $3$:
$$
\frac{45}{12}=\frac{15}{4}
$$
So the length changes $15$ units for every $4$ units of width change.

steps.title: Scale the width increase
steps.content: The width increases by $8$ units, which is $2$ groups of $4$. The length must increase by $2$ groups of $15$: $$ 2\cdot 15=30 $$ The answer is B.
